clean_text:
NATIONAL HIGHWAYS AUTHORITY OF INDIA
REGIONAL OFFICE   KARNATAKA & GOA
4th Floor, Samruddhi Bhavan, Palace Road, Bengaluru   560 001
NOTICE INVITING TENDER (NIT)
NIT No.: NHAI/RO-KGO/NH-66/HC-03/2026-27/01
Date: 12 June 2026
TENDER FOR CONSTRUCTION OF 4-LANE DIVIDED CARRIAGEWAY ON NH-66
(Panaji to Udupi Section   Package HC-03)
Chainage: Km 182.000 to Km 231.600 (49.6 km)
1. WORK DESCRIPTION
National Highways Authority of India (NHAI), Regional Office Karnataka & Goa,
invites sealed tenders from eligible contractors for the following work:
Project Name: Four-Laning of NH-66 Panaji Udupi Section (Package HC-03)
Chainage    : Km 182.000 to Km 231.600 (49.6 km), Karnataka
Scope       : Earthwork, pavement construction (WMM, DBM, BC), drainage,
              bridges (3 Nos.), culverts (42 Nos.), retaining walls,
              road signage, safety barriers, and service roads.
Estimated Cost  : Rs. 4,50,00,00,000/- (Rs. Four Hundred Fifty Crore)
EMD             : Rs. 90,00,000/- (Rs. Ninety Lakh) (2% of estimated cost)
Tender Fee      : Rs. 25,000/- (non-refundable, payable by DD)
Performance Security : 5% of contract value
Defect Liability Period : 5 years
2. KEY DATES
Publication of NIT              : 12 June 2026
Last date for clarifications    : 25 June 2026
Pre-Bid Meeting                 : 27 June 2026, 11:00 hrs at NHAI RO Bengaluru
Last date for bid submission    : 15 July 2026, 17:00 hrs
Opening of Technical Bids       : 17 July 2026, 11:00 hrs
Bid validity period             : 180 days from date of submission
3. ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA
IMPORTANT: All eligibility criteria must be fulfilled as on the last date of
bid submission. Non-fulfilment of any mandatory criterion shall lead to
summary rejection of the bid.
3.1 Annual Turnover

The applicant (or lead member in case of JV) shall demonstrate:
  Annual Turnover not less than Rs. 150.00 Crore in each of the last three
  (3) financial years (2023-24, 2024-25, 2025-26) from civil/highway works.
  Supporting documents: Audited Balance Sheets and Profit & Loss statements
  for the last 3 financial years, certified by a Chartered Accountant.
3.2 Technical Experience   Similar Highway Works
The applicant shall have successfully completed at least 2 similar highway
works during the last 7 (seven) years (on or after 01 April 2019), each
not less than Rs. 75.00 Crore.
  A "similar work" is defined as: Construction or rehabilitation of a
  National Highway or State Highway with a 4-lane divided carriageway,
  including flexible pavement (DBM + BC) and associated structures.
  Supporting documents: Performance/completion certificate from the client
  authority, specifying the scope, value, and date of completion.
  Note: Work orders for ongoing works or works completed before 01 April 2019
  shall not be considered.
3.3 Net Worth
The Net Worth shall not be less than Rs. 50.00 Crore as at the close of the
preceding financial year (2025-26).
  Net Worth = Paid-up Capital + Free Reserves   Intangible Assets   Losses
  Supporting documents: Audited Balance Sheet, certified by CA.
3.4 Certifications
The bidder shall hold the following valid certifications:
  (a) ISO 9001:2015   Quality Management System
  (b) ISO 14001:2015   Environmental Management System
  (c) ISO 45001:2018   Occupational Health & Safety Management System
  Copies of valid certificates to be submitted. Expired certificates
  shall not be accepted.
3.5 Registrations
  (a) Valid GST Registration (GSTIN)
  (b) PAN
  (c) EPF & ESIC Registration
  (d) NHAI Empanelment as Class-I Contractor (or equivalent MORT&H Category)
4. JOINT VENTURE CONDITIONS

4.1 JV Eligibility
In case of a Joint Venture:
  - Maximum 3 partners
  - Lead member must hold at least 40% equity
  - Lead member must individually meet 60% of all financial criteria
  - Each JV partner must meet 25% of financial criteria individually
4.2 JV Restrictions
  A firm may be member of only one JV bidding for this tender.
  A firm participating as individual bidder cannot also be a JV partner.
5. SUBMISSION OF BIDS
5.1 Two-Cover System
Bids shall be submitted in two covers:
  Cover 1   Technical Bid:
    - EMD (bank guarantee / demand draft)
    - Eligibility documents (Sections 3.1 to 3.5 above)
    - Performance certificates for qualifying works
    - Audited financial statements
    - Organisational structure and key personnel CVs
    - JV agreement (if applicable)
  Cover 2   Financial Bid:
    - Schedule of quantities with unit rates (Bill of Quantities)
    - Price bid in prescribed format
5.2 Submission Mode
Bids must be submitted online on NHAI e-procurement portal
(https://nhai.etenders.gov.in) before the deadline.
Hard copies of EMD and tender fee DD must reach NHAI RO Bengaluru
by 17 July 2026, 17:00 hrs.
6. SCOPE OF WORK   SUMMARY
6.1 Pavement Works
  - Earthwork (embankment and cutting): approx. 28 lakh cum
  - Granular Sub-Base (GSB): 2.4 lakh cum
  - Wet Mix Macadam (WMM): 1.9 lakh cum
  - Dense Bituminous Macadam (DBM), 50 mm: 1.2 lakh MT
  - Bituminous Concrete (BC), 40 mm: 0.8 lakh MT
6.2 Structures

  - Major Bridges: 3 Nos. (total span: 420 m)
  - Minor Bridges: 12 Nos.
  - Box Culverts: 42 Nos.
  - Retaining Walls (MSE): 8 Nos. (total length: 2,400 m)
  - Underpasses (vehicular): 6 Nos.
  - Pedestrian subways: 4 Nos.
6.3 Safety & Signage
  - W-beam crash barriers: 95 km (both sides)
  - Thermoplastic road markings: complete package
  - Retroreflective sign boards: as per IRC SP-55
6.4 Utilities Shifting
  Co-ordination with KPTCL (electricity), BSNL (telecom), water supply
  lines for shifting, as per alignment requirements.
7. CONTRACT CONDITIONS
7.1 Time for Completion: 36 months from the date of commencement
7.2 Liquidated Damages: 0.05% of contract value per week, maximum 10%
7.3 Price Escalation: As per Ministry of Road Transport & Highways (MoRTH)
    price adjustment clause applicable to NHAI contracts.
7.4 Mobilisation Advance: 10% of contract value, to be recovered from
    running account bills.
7.5 Dispute Resolution: Arbitration as per NHAI standard conditions
    of contract (SCC), in accordance with Arbitration & Conciliation
    Act, 1996.
8. GENERAL CONDITIONS
8.1 NHAI reserves the right to reject any or all bids without assigning
    any reason.
8.2 Canvassing in any form shall lead to disqualification.
8.3 The successful bidder must deploy a Resident Project Manager with
    minimum 15 years of highway/expressway project experience.
8.4 NHAI is not responsible for any postal/transit delays.
8.5 All disputes are subject to jurisdiction of courts in New Delhi.

9. CONTACT DETAILS
Project Director (Technical)
NHAI Regional Office   Karnataka & Goa
4th Floor, Samruddhi Bhavan, Palace Road
Bengaluru   560 001
Email: pd-tech.ro-kgo@nhai.gov.in
Phone: 080-2238-4455
END OF NOTICE INVITING TENDER
NIT No. NHAI/RO-KGO/NH-66/HC-03/2026-27/01
